rewrite_detail.reason: Flag word: 'melancholic' adds sorrow-bias to an architectural image. 'Reflective' preserves the contemplative documentary tone without conditioning AI toward heavier grief imagery. The UNESCO tension (S013 scene purpose) is better served by a still, factual beauty than sorrow-coded atmosphere.

batch_2a_images.historical_sensitivity_note: Scene depicts personal belongings of the 5,000+ civilian residents of Hashima who evacuated in 1974. This is NOT forced labor imagery — it documents the civilian community. The child's shoe and items are documented in historical accounts of the evacuation. Respectful documentary treatment confirmed.
batch_2a_images.critical_output_qa_instruction: After generation: inspect the 'old photograph' prop in the image. If ANY human face is visible or partially visible in the photograph (even fictional, even blurred): REJECT immediately and regenerate. The protection phrase 'too damaged and faded to show any identifiable features' is already in the positive prompt. Negative prompt has been expanded to cover this. Max 3 regeneration attempts. If 3 attempts all produce a visible face in the photograph: remove the photograph prop from the prompt and regenerate with shoe + can only.

audit_result.victims: PASS — objects document civilian residents, not victims of violence
audit_result.suffering: PASS — 'mood of memory and hasty departure' refers to 1974 evacuation, not suffering
audit_result.forced_labor: PASS — S016 is ACT_III (7:00-7:30), well after the forced labor narration section. Personal items belong to the civilian community era.
audit_result.ghost_horror: PASS — 'mood of memory' is documentary, not supernatural
audit_result.misleading_historical_simulation: PASS_WITH_NOTE — the child's shoe and items are symbolic documentary props representing documented historical reality. The faded photograph must not simulate a real person. The protection language is in place.
audit_result.respectful_tone: PASS — these items represent the community, not victims. Tone is memorial, not exploitative.

batch_2a_images.reason_approved_for_batch_2a: Close-up still life of abandoned personal items representing the civilian residents of Hashima (evacuated 1974). The child's shoe, faded photograph, and rusted can are documented elements of the historical record. S016 scene (ACT_III, 7:00-7:30) follows the mine closure narration — this is the 'community that lived here' moment, not a forced labor reference. The prompt contains strong built-in protection for the photograph prop ('too damaged and faded to show any identifiable features'). Negative prompt expanded to cover faces in photograph prop specifically. Critical output QA gate required.
